title: Chain of Thought Prompting
learning_outcomes: Explain Chain of Thought (CoT) logical splits.

content_sections.title: Reasoning Token Allocation
content_sections.content: Chain of Thought (CoT) prompting forces models to output intermediate reasoning steps before generating a final answer. This allocates more compute to reasoning: because the model is autoregressive, each generated token allows the attention layers to compute more complex representations.

content_sections.title: Self-Consistency Voting Metrics
content_sections.content:
To resolve reasoning drift, **Self-Consistency** samples multiple generation paths ($N$ paths) using temperature sampling, and applies a majority vote to select the most common final answer:

$$\text{Answer}^* = \arg\max_{a} \sum_{i=1}^N \mathbb{I}\left( \text{extract}(path_i) = a \right)$$

This mitigates single-path reasoning errors, improving accuracy on complex logic and mathematics tasks.
